subject: Re: WarpTools and AMD GPUs
body:
If anyone is willing to fund the porting of all CUDA kernels to ROCm (or 
whatever is AMD's currently favored stack) and figuring out their 
co-existence, we'll be happy to merge the code :-)

On Thursday, December 5, 2024 at 3:34:08 PM UTC-8 alist...@gmail.com wrote:

> Hi Juha,
>
> There are many CUDA kernels 
> <https://github.com/warpem/warp/tree/main/NativeAcceleration/gtom/src>, 
> PyTorch is only used for deep learning in Warp.
>
> Cheers,
>
> Alister
>
> On Thu, 5 Dec 2024 at 12:38, Juha Huiskonen <juha.hu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>
>> Hi Alister,
>>
>> Would this work if I install PyTorch with ROCm support for AMD GPUs?
>> Or is there something in the code that is tied directly to CUDA?
>>
>> Cheers,
>> Juha
>
>
>>
>> On Thu, Dec 5, 2024 at 4:38 PM Alister Burt <alist...@gmail.com> wrote:
>> >
>> > Hi Juha,
>> >
>> > It’s not impossible in principle but the work required to make it 
>> happen hasn’t been done and isn’t planned.
>> >
>> > Cheers,
>> >
>> > Alister
>> >
>> > Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
>> >
>> > On Dec 4, 2024, at 22:31, Juha Huiskonen <juha.hu...@gmail.com> wrote:
>> >
>> > ﻿Dear all,
>> >
>> > Is it possible to run WarpTools and MCore using AMD GPUs.  Specifically 
>> AMD MI250x?
>> >
>> > Cheers,
>> > Juha
>> >
>> > --
>> > You received this message because you are subscribed to the Google 
>> Groups "Warp" group.
>> > To unsubscribe from this group and stop receiving emails from it, send 
>> an email to warp-em+u...@googlegroups.com.
>> > To view this discussion visit 
>> https://groups.google.com/d/msgid/warp-em/4dc8875b-0aa1-4537-b627-95aa5d81cf78n%40googlegroups.com
>> .
>>
>
